revision.text:
[[File:Suyama_kofun_stereo.jpg|thumb|270x270px|Suyama Kofun ]] {{Coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|display=title}}'''Suyama Kofun''' ({{Lang-ja|巣山古墳}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Added a source.
revision.text:
[[File:Suyama_kofun_stereo.jpg|thumb|270x270px|Suyama Kofun ]] {{Coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|display=title}}'''Suyama Kofun''' ({{Lang-ja|巣山古墳}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> It has a length of about 204 metres.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> As of 2000, it was one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]], as it is not thought to be directly connected to [[Imperial House of Japan|the Imperial line]].<ref name=":0" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also ==
* [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] {{Japan-stub}} [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]]

[[File:Suyama_kofun_stereo.jpg|thumb|270x270px|Suyama Kofun ]] {{Coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|display=title}}'''Suyama Kofun''' ({{Lang-ja|巣山古墳}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> It has a length of about 204 metres.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> As of 2000, it was one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 metres in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]], as it is not thought to be directly connected to [[Imperial House of Japan|the Imperial line]].<ref name=":0" />

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 790911 Suyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in [[Kōryō, Nara]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national [[Special Historic Site]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the [[Imperial Household Agency]].<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the [[Meiji period]], and most of the many [[grave goods]] are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG|Group of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
