{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suyama Kofun]][[en:Suyama Kofun]][[ja:巣山古墳]][[zh:巢山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suyama Kofun]][[en:Suyama Kofun]][[ja:巣山古墳]][[zh:巢山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suyama Kofun]][[en:Suyama Kofun]][[ja:巣山古墳]][[zh:巢山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11479418]] Wikidata name: Suyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Suyama kofun stereo.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556389|N|135.740833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}

==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Suyama Kofun}} *[https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=28&frmCd=8-1-0-0-0 Kōryō Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Suyama Kofun}} *[https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=28&frmCd=8-1-0-0-0 Kōryō Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line}}.

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Suyama kofun stereo.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556389|N|135.740833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station|1=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line|1=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Suyama Kofun}} *[https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=28&frmCd=8-1-0-0-0 Kōryō Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.

It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Suyama kofun stereo.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556389|N|135.740833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station|1=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line|1=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Suyama Kofun}} *[https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=28&frmCd=8-1-0-0-0 Kōryō Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station|1=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line|1=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Suyama Kofun}} *[https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=28&frmCd=8-1-0-0-0 Kōryō Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.

It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station|1=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line|1=Tawaramoto Line}}.

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] | name = Suyama Kofun | native_name =巣山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Suyama kofun stereo.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Suyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Suyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556389|N|135.740833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|23|N|135|44|27|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Suyama Kofun'''|巣山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Miyoshi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース {{!}} 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース「いかす・なら」|url=http://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00243.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.pref.nara.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kofun]] that is located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳 文化遺産オンライン|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163321|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=観光 巣山古墳概要 {{!}} 広陵町|url=https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=kak&frmId=28|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.town.koryo.nara.jp}}</ref> It is designated as a national {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=小項目事典|first=国指定史跡ガイド,精選版 日本国語大辞典,百科事典マイペディア,知恵蔵,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|title=巣山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B7%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-85101|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1959|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=巣山古墳（すやまこふん）|url=https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0425.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.library.pref.nara.jp}}</ref> The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, with its status elevated to that of a Special National Historic Site in 1952.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It the central tumulus of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster and is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century.
==Overview== The Suyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is facing north, but its central axis faces slightly northeast. A wide moat surrounds the mound, which is completely filled with water. The mound is built of earth in three tiers, and the slope of each tier steeper than the one before it. The slopes are covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of andesite-based gravel and broken stones. Traces of rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. Square projections are attached to the narrow part connecting the anterior and posterior sections. The total length of the tumulus is 202 meters,<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Edwards|first=Walter|date=2000|title=Contested Access: The Imperial Tombs in the Postwar Period|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133273|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=26|issue=2|pages=375|doi=10.2307/133273|issn=0095-6848}}</ref> making it one of a small handful of ''kofun'' tombs over 200 meters in length which were not administered by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref name=":0" /> Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s evealed a base stone 8 meters wide outside the base of the mound, revising its total length to 220 meters. The posterior circular mound has a diameter of 130 meters, and height of 25 meters; the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 112 meters, and height of 21 meters. The top of the circular mound is flat with a diameter of 45 meters. The moat is shield-shaped, and the width is 33 meters at the circular mound side, 37 meters at the front, and 57 meters around the narrow part. The outer bank of the moat is 27 meters wide at its widest point, and is built on parts of the south, east, and north sides, and is particularly high and wide on the east and northeast sides. Two pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s parallel to the main axis of the circular mound were excavated in the circular section, but they were looted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and most of the many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are now lost.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
<gallery> Suyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Suyama Kofun 巣山古墳出土 喪船.JPG|Mourning ship excavated from Suyama Tomb 巣山古墳 出土埴輪.JPG| part_of of excavated ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 水鳥形埴輪.JPG|waterfowl-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 囲形埴輪.JPG|Enclosure-shaped ''haniwa'' 巣山古墳出土 柵形埴輪.JPG|Fence-shaped ''haniwa'' </gallery>
The Suyama Kofun is approximately four kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station|1=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line|1=Tawaramoto Line}}.
